purpose: Progressive coarse-to-fine design protocol. The design is never generated in one shot: each pass receives only the minimum context slice (micro-pack) it needs, so a local model stays within a stable load budget while engineering judgment is applied at every level of detail.
principle: Outer frame first, then infiltrate inward: skeleton -> per-subsystem detail -> interface reconciliation -> integration scoring. A later pass may only refine what an earlier pass established; it may not silently restructure it.

passes.name: skeleton
passes.output: Top-level part_tree with one node per required_subsystem, global datum scheme, overall envelope, and stub adjacent_interfaces between subsystems. No detailed geometry_ops allowed.

passes.name: subsystem_detail
input: interface summaries of adjacent subsystems only (no foreign geometry)
passes.output: For this subsystem only: P0 plan -> part_tree children -> 12-24 coordinate-bearing geometry_ops, with every evidence_feature represented.

passes.name: interface_reconcile
input: adjacent_interfaces lists of all subsystems (geometry stripped)
passes.output: List of interface mismatches (datum disagreement, missing mate, clearance conflict) with a fix instruction per mismatch, routed back as PASS-1 reruns for the affected subsystems only.

passes.name: integrate_and_score
passes.output: Scorecard: subsystem completeness check (missing required_subsystem or evidence_feature = failure finding), per-criterion scores, and aggregated loop_feedback for the next evolution cycle.

load_rules.context_budget_is_hard_limit: If a micro-pack exceeds max_input_kb, drop extracts first, then local_reading_only listings; never drop criteria or evidence_features.
load_rules.carry_forward: Only the skeleton tree and interface summaries cross pass boundaries; raw geometry of other subsystems never enters a subsystem call.
